input.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

payload.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

source_payload.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

pinterest_cluster_labels: light-wood Japandi dining — round table + woven pendant
floor-level tea nook — floor cushions + bamboo screens
neutral Japandi living — low sofa, sculptural wooden coffee table

pins.title: Japandi Dining Room with Neutral Decor
pins.description: A Japandi dining room with neutral décor blends Japanese simplicity and Scandinavian warmth. Soft beige, warm wood, and gentle earth tones create a calm, inviting space. Clean lines, natural materials, and minimal furnishings keep the room feeling open and uncluttered, while subtle textures like linen, ceramic, and light wood add quiet elegance and comfort.
pins.cluster_label: light-wood Japandi dining — round table + woven pendant

pins.title: Japandi nook area
pins.cluster_label: floor-level tea nook — floor cushions + bamboo screens

pins.title: Japandi Living Room: Calm Minimal Decor, Natural Wood & Indoor Plants
pins.description: Calm Japandi living room inspiration with a light neutral sofa, low wooden coffee table, soft textured rug, and plenty of indoor plants for a serene, balanced space. Minimal wall art, natural materials, and clean lines create an airy, soothing aesthetic—perfect for Japanese style interiors, minimalist living room ideas, and warm neutral home decor. Save for Japandi decor and plant-filled living room styling.
pins.cluster_label: neutral Japandi living — low sofa, sculptural wooden coffee table

cluster_summary.label: light-wood Japandi dining — round table + woven pendant
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Round oak dining table, curved mid-height upholstered chairs in linen textures, woven rattan pendant for warm upward glow; staging uses ceramic tableware, single sculptural vase and subtle wall wood slatting to elongate vertical lines.

cluster_summary.label: floor-level tea nook — floor cushions + bamboo screens
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Intimate floor seating with oversized cushioned loungers, layered natural fiber rugs and bamboo/shoji screening; low wooden tray table, ambient paper lanterns and small green plantings to create a cozy, meditative corner.

cluster_summary.label: neutral Japandi living — low sofa, sculptural wooden coffee table
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Light neutral linen sofa on low wooden base, asymmetrical sculpted oak coffee table, textured area rug and potted greenery; pared-back art and vertical wood slat accents emphasize calm proportion and organic materiality.

vibe_report.summary: A warm, calming Japandi interior that balances Scandinavian lightness with Japanese restraint. Neutral warm-beige palette, low-profile furniture, natural materials (light oak, rattan, linen), and layered texture create a quiet, family-friendly home. Prioritize a compact work corner in the living area, a safe crib placement in the bedroom, and uncluttered circulation around bay windows and patio doors. Lighting should read as daylight from existing windows and supplemented by practical fixtures (woven pendant, floor lamp) only where visible in-frame.
vibe_report.palette_rationale: Warm neutrals (soft beiges, sand, muted greys) with a deeper warm-brown accent to ground compositions. This keeps skin tones natural, wood grain visible, and textures readable on camera while remaining calming and consistent with Japandi pins.
vibe_report.lighting_mood: Soft ambient daylight with directional shaping from bay windows and patio glass. Use slightly sculpted shadows to add depth (editorial, not theatrical). Visible practical fixtures like a woven rattan pendant or paper lantern provide warm fill in low-light zones.
materials: light oak (tables, shelving, low sofa bases)
ceramic tableware and matte stoneware surfaces
muted plaster or warm white painted walls
furniture_language: low-profile seating with visible wooden bases
rounded table edges and gentle curves
tapered, exposed legs for visual lightness
modular storage and open shelving for curated objects
compact standing desk with minimal silhouette
styling_rules: limit palette to 3-4 neutrals plus one warm-dark accent for contrast
curate shelves — 6–10 objects per 6ft shelf, keep negative space
prioritize low furniture heights to preserve sight lines to windows
place the standing desk near natural light, keep it visually light (open-legged)
crib must be placed against interior wall away from direct window glare and cords
use plants sparingly (1–2 medium and a few small) to introduce life without clutter
avoid: bright saturated colors or busy patterns
tall, high-backed furniture that breaks Japandi scale
artificial light sources that contradict window direction
over-styling with too many small decorative objects

room_guidance.headline: Low, airy Japandi living with a compact work corner
room_guidance.guidance: Frame the living area as an open, calm gathering space with a low linen sofa facing the bay/patio, a sculptural oak coffee table, and a compact standing desk tucked into the window corner to form a subtle work nook. Use an open bookshelf as a visual divider between the desk and lounge but keep shelving sparse and curated. Emphasize front windows and patio glass as the primary light source. Add a single woven pendant or floor lamp for evening fill only if visible in reference photo.
must_avoid: placing standing desk in center of room
blocking patio doors/windows
heavy patterned textiles

room_guidance.headline: Calm bedroom with safe crib placement and soft materials
room_guidance.guidance: Maintain a restful composition: low bed platform, soft linen bedding, and a small crib positioned against an interior wall away from direct window glare. Keep visual weight to one side with a simple dresser or open shelving on the opposite wall. Use soft ambient side lighting (table lamp) derived from visible fixtures only. Preserve medium northern light as the key directional source.
must_avoid: crib under direct window or in front of radiator
tall dressers that cut vertical sightlines
bright colorful bedding

room_guidance.headline: Simple, functional kitchen with visual connection to dining bay
room_guidance.guidance: Stage the kitchen to read as warm and efficient: light wood lower cabinets, neutral counters, minimal visible appliances, and an open pass toward the dining bay. Highlight material junctions (wood counter edge, ceramic backsplash) and keep the sightline to the dining bay clear. Derive any additional light from the bay/dining windows and visible overhead fixtures only.
must_avoid: introducing new directional lights not present in frame
excessive countertop styling

room_guidance.headline: Light-wood Japandi dining bay with woven pendant focal point
room_guidance.guidance: Center a round light-oak dining table beneath a woven rattan pendant. Use curved, mid-height chairs in neutral linen. Keep the bay windows unobstructed and use the pendant as a visible practical light source. Place a single sculptural vase or ceramic set on the table to anchor composition.
must_avoid: overcrowding the bay with plants or tall objects
using a pendant that is too large or too modern/metallic

room_guidance.headline: Sunroom patio with floor-level seating and natural screens
room_guidance.guidance: Create a calm sunroom: low seating or floor cushions, layered natural rugs, and a bamboo/shoji screen if privacy is needed. Keep large patio glass clear and readable; let exterior light be the dominant source. Use one practical paper lantern or floor lamp visible in-frame for evening shots.
must_avoid: blocking the glass with tall furniture
introducing non-Japandi bright decor

warnings: Floor plan-derived window positions are partially inferred—confirm exact window/door locations in photography to preserve visible-light-source logic.
Small service rooms (pantry, closets) are deprioritized for cinematic passes; keep these static photographic shots to avoid motion artifacts.

creative_spec.overall_mood: Quiet, meditative warmth — airy daylight with tactile materiality and restrained, family-friendly staging.
global_style_notes: Preserve all architectural elements and room geometry; do not alter door/window placements or add openings.
Derive all lighting from visible sources only (bay windows, patio glass, visible pendants/lamps).
Favor low-angle, wide compositions that emphasize horizontal lines and low furniture heights typical of Japandi.
Use minimal, curated props to suggest family life (a small stack of children's books on the shelf, a folded knit blanket, a single ceramic cup) without cluttering frames.
For night/evening variants, rely on visible practicals (pendants, floor lamps) and keep color temperature warm (2700–3000K) consistent across shots.
creative_spec.negative_prompt: blur, distort, low quality, warped architecture, flicker, unstable camera, unrealistic lighting, muddy shadows, layout drift

warnings.message: Confirm exact window and door locations from photos before running image-to-video passes — floor-plan-inferred windows may cause inconsistent light direction if not verified.
Run at most two image-refinement passes prior to video generation to avoid drifting composition or over-stylization; default mode recommended: raw-photo -> image pass -> video.
Suppress any prompt language that asks the model to alter architecture, move walls/doors, or introduce new openings.
Floor plan-derived window positions are partially inferred—confirm exact window/door locations in photography to preserve visible-light-source logic.
Small service rooms (pantry, closets) are deprioritized for cinematic passes; keep these static photographic shots to avoid motion artifacts.
